other_observers: none; Bill Prather found and identified the Glossy Ibis and hybrids.
specific_location: Grassy meadow on the north shore of Union Reservoir
description: On April 24, 2010 I observed one adult Glossy Ibis with at least two hybrid Glossy x White-faced Ibis and ~ 100 White-faced Ibis in a grassy meadow on the north shore of Union Reservoir in Weld County, Colorado. The birds had been first found by Bill Prather the previous day.   The pure Glossy Ibis had the characteristic blue facial skin boarder, had a dark brown iris, and it lacked any white feathering on the face. The blue facial skin did not wrap around the back of the eye. The legs were not particularly bright, but had noticeable pinkish knees. The bill was an even pale gray. Overall, there was not much of a difference in plumage coloration between the Glossy and adjacent White-faced Ibis.Behaviors: The bird fed actively during most of the 1 hour of observation. The flock took off two or three times because of passing traffic, but generally they were rather comfortable and allowed prolonged study. When flushed, they would quickly settle back down, one even landing in the middle of the road. The behavior of the Glossy Ibis was no different from any of the White-faced Ibis. No antagonistic behavior was noted.Call: nonePlumage: Adult
photographer: Photographer: Christian Nunes, Christian Nunes, Christian Nunes, Christian Nunes
